calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Continued To Feb-01-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Code-Compliant Verifications
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ALEJANDRO GARZON's, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Code-Compliant Verifications.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.
The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to compel code-compliant verifications/denied.
1. First, the motion is moot.
a. Defendant served an amended verification that substantially complies with CCP 2015.5. (Culp Decl., par. 9; Ex. 2.)
b. The court notes that "the object of a verification is to assure good faith in the averments or statements of a party." (Frio v. Superior Crt. (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1480, 1498.)
c. Where there is no allegation of bad faith, a claim of inadequate verification is without merit. (See League of Women Voters v. Eu (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 649, 656.)
d. "The defect [improper verification] is one of form which does not affect the substance." (Soltani-Rastegar v. Sup. Crt. (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 424, 428.)

2. The motion is also untimely.
a. "As with any issue of statutory interpretation, [w]e begin ... with the statute's actual words, the most reliable indicator of legislative intent, assigning them their usual and ordinary meanings, and construing them in context.
b. If the words themselves are not ambiguous, we presume the Legislature meant what it said, and the statute's plain meaning governs.
c. On the other hand, if the language allows more than one reasonable construction, we may look to such aids as the legislative history of the measure and maxims of statutory construction.
d. In cases of uncertain meaning, we may also consider the consequences of a particular interpretation, including its impact on public policy." (Golf And Tennis Pro Shop Inc. v. Superior Court (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 127, 134-135 (cleaned up).) "We are not to 'construe statutes in isolation, but rather read every statute with reference to the entire scheme of law of which it is part so that the whole may be harmonized and retain effectiveness.'" (Id. at 136.)

3. Under CCP 2031.310(c), plaintiff has 45-days from service of the "verified response" to seek relief.
a. Plaintiff missed that deadline by months. (Culp Decl., par. 5.)
b. The fact that the verification contained defects does not eclipse the fact that defendant provided a "verified response."
c. In addition, this factual scenario presents a perfect example for a meet and confer prior to judicial intervention.
d. Treating this motion as a motion to compel further responses advances the meet and confer requirement of the Discovery Act.
4. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $1,000. This motion lacked substantial justification since a simple phone call could have resolved the matter.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/BZ)
